channel.title: Latest alerts from washingtonpost.com
channel.description: An unofficial feed created by Derek Willis.

item.title: Energy prices. ICE at airports. LaGuardia collision. Columbus statue. Casey Means. Cancer risks. Aging mistakes. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: Trump threats, U.S. troop build-up raise specter of battle for Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Israeli officials said securing the strait for energy shipments could become the war’s main goal now that regime change and ending Iran’s ability to obtain a nuclear weapon seem out of reach.

item.title: Trump installs Christopher Columbus statue on White House grounds
item.description: The installation marks President Donald Trump's latest effort to remake the White House campus. The piece is a reconstruction of a statue that protesters dumped into Baltimore's harbor in 2020.

item.title: A luau’s last night shows Hawaii’s changing tourist tastes
item.description: Paradise Cove entertained thousands of guests and employed generations of locals over the decades. But the nature of tourism in the state is changing.

item.title: Four truths about covid that have become clouded over time
item.description: In the early days, the virus posed a graver threat to people and the health care system, President Donald Trump embraced lockdowns he now blasts, and the benefits of vaccines were oversold.

item.title: Why Iran does not appear ready to give in, despite heavy losses
item.description: Tehran’s unwillingness to capitulate as the war enters its fourth week is wrapped up in the power it exerts over the Strait of Hormuz, officials in the region say.

item.title: As Trump says ICE will deploy to airports Monday, border czar says he is working to identify what agents will do and where
item.description: A month into the government funding standoff that is straining airports, Tom Homan said Sunday that ICE agents "will be at the airports tomorrow helping TSA move those lines along" but will not be involved in X-ray screening or other work that requires specialized training.

item.title: Still need to finish this weekend’s On the Record? We’ve got you.
item.description: Play now to finish out your score for the week.

item.title: One gas station, two drivers, three fill-ups: The Iran war hits this state especially hard
item.description: In Colorado, gasoline prices that keep climbing have changed daily lives for many. But the effects are not equally felt.

item.title: I want to avoid the ‘aging mistakes’ my parents made. I’ve got work to do, our columnist writes.
item.description: Twenty years ago, contributor Steven Petrow started a list of what he considered “stupid things” that old people do. Now, he’s seeing his life, and his list, in a different light.

item.title: Crossword and a coffee: Solve the Sunday puzzle now
item.description: Crossword and a coffee: Solve the Sunday puzzle now

item.title: Trump threatens to strike Iran’s power plants if the country does not fully open the Strait of Hormuz within 48 hours
item.description: In a Saturday night post on Truth Social, the president said the U.S. would “obliterate” Iran’s power plants if it did not fully open the waterway “without threat … from this exact point in time.” Energy prices have continued to spike during the war, with Tehran having largely shut down the vital shipping chokepoint.

item.title: Column: Congress could be headed for a tipping point and an irreversible decline
item.description: Critics are sounding the alarm that the institution needs a major course correction or else it will slide into a permanent state of weakness that further empowers the presidency, columnist Paul Kane writes.

item.title: A massive border wall expansion is underway. Pushback is coming from all sides.
item.description: The aggressive pace of the project has alarmed advocates who say the construction will destroy pristine country, threaten endangered species, and cut off access to sacred Indigenous and archaeological sites.

item.title: A teen’s traumatic path from bullying and abuse to Trump’s State of the Union
item.description: Sage Blair ran away — from bullies, from school officials who did not tell family of her gender transition, from parents who didn’t believe a person can change genders. Rape, a lawsuit and an invitation from the president followed.

item.title: Trump threatens to deploy ICE to airports as TSA shortages drive delays
item.description: Deploying agents to checkpoints nationwide would mark an unprecedented expansion of immigration enforcement even as Democrats push for tighter limits on how those agents operate, citing concerns that the administration has fast-tracked training to expand ICE ranks.

item.title: Robert Mueller has died at 81. He became a political target as special counsel probing Russian interference in the 2016 election.
item.description: At the FBI, in an era of increasingly partisan national division, he built a reputation for nonpartisan rectitude and stone-faced reserve. Then, in his last major role on the national stage, Mr. Mueller found himself at the center of a political tempest.

item.title: To tilt Hungarian election, Russians proposed staging an assassination attempt
item.description: To aid Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban, a friend of Russia, in his election, operatives proposed “the Gamechanger” — a staged assassination attempt to stir supporters.

item.title: TSA lines are miserably long. Don’t miss your flight because of them.
item.description: The partial government shutdown is straining security lines at airports. Some tips: Monitor security checkpoint wait times, register for expedited security programs and invoke the “flat-tire” rule.

item.title: How many guesses will Keyword take you today? Try to beat the average now.
item.description: How many guesses will Keyword take you today? Try to beat the average now.

item.title: These car brands could suffer the most with soaring gas prices. Look up your vehicle.
item.description: A Post analysis quantifies the pain at the pump as the Iran war drives the nationwide average gas price to $3.91 — the highest in almost four years.

item.title: U.S. lifts sanctions on millions of barrels of oil from Iran, in a move likely to provide revenue for the country’s war effort
item.description: The Treasury Department’s authorization to temporarily permit the sale of Iranian oil comes in response to a global energy crunch triggered by President Donald Trump’s war in Iran. The price of oil has increased more than 50 percent since the U.S. attacked Iran late last month.

item.title: Cuba rejects ‘shameless’ U.S. Embassy request to bring in fuel during Trump administration’s blockade on the island
item.description: The denial could force the embassy to order nonessential staff to leave Cuba in May or “possibly earlier,” the embassy warned Wednesday in a cable to the State Department. Cuba has received no oil imports for the past three months, President Miguel Díaz-Canel said last week.

item.title: Pentagon’s restrictions on reporters ruled unconstitutional in case brought by New York Times
item.description: The policy stated that the Pentagon could revoke the credentials of any journalist who solicited information that the department did not explicitly authorize for release — even if that information was unclassified. A federal judge found that it violated the First and Fifth Amendments.

item.title: Justice Dept. seeks to drop remaining charges against two officers connected to Breonna Taylor raid
item.description: Taylor, a Black woman, was shot and killed by Louisville police who were carrying out a search warrant at her apartment in March 2020. Federal authorities later brought civil rights charges against officers tied to the deadly raid, accusing two of them — Joshua Jaynes and Kyle Meany — of knowingly using false information to obtain the warrant used to justify the search.

item.title: House Republicans subpoena Virginia prosecutor over activist who protested Stephen Miller
item.description: The demand comes after Rep. Jim Jordan, chairman of the House Judiciary Committee, opened an inquiry into Parisa Dehghani-Tafti, Arlington County’s lead prosecutor.

item.title: Thousands have swooned over this MAGA dream girl. She’s made with AI.
item.description: A viral fake of an Army service member spotlights a new trend in online attention harvesting: part patriotism, part porn and 100 percent computer-made.

item.title: Trump administration sues Harvard, alleging the school did not do enough to protect Jewish students from harassment
item.description: The Justice Department lawsuit filed Friday is the second time the administration has sued Harvard in as many months.

item.title: CBS News will end radio service, cut more jobs as top editor Bari Weiss struggles to right ship
item.description: The troubled network is eliminating its century-old CBS News Radio division and laying off more staff.

item.title: Chuck Norris, ‘Walker, Texas Ranger’ star, dead at 86
item.description: The actor and martial arts star passed away on Thursday, his family confirmed in a post on social media.

item.title: César Chavez and the lies the left tells itself. I know them well.
item.description: Women and girls sexually abused by progressive heroes are discouraged from speaking up.

item.title: 10 questions, and don't forget the bonus wager! Finish off your week with On the Record.
item.description: Think you’ve got what it takes to earn a perfect score? Take today’s longer news quiz and complete your point total for the week.

item.title: U.S.-Israeli rift widens over potential end game in Iran
item.description: Israel’s assault on the world’s largest natural gas deposit has renewed tensions between the two allies over their war aims in Iran.

item.title: Iran conflict. Pearl Harbor. Free speech. Jan. 6 plaque. César Chavez. ‘The Bachelorette.’ Gen Z pout. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: U.S. approves $6.2 billion Nexstar-Tegna merger set to reshape local TV
item.description: The Justice Department and the Federal Communications Commission each gave a green light to the deal, Nexstar said. FCC Chairman Brendan Carr let the deal proceed by waiving a cap that has historically barred station owners from expanding to reach more than 39 percent of American households. Nexstar previously said the deal would give it 265 stations across the country, reaching 80 percent of households.

item.title: Airfares are spiking. Here’s how to navigate the hikes.
item.description: The war in Iran, now in its third week, is causing oil prices to soar, saddling airlines with some of the highest fuel costs in years.

item.title: Trump administration moves key student loan oversight from Education Department to Treasury
item.description: Transferring the oversight of defaulted student loans is a significant step toward dismantling the Education Department.

item.title: ‘Bachelorette’ season canceled after video appears to show star Taylor Frankie Paul committing domestic violence
item.description: The “Secret Lives of Mormon Wives” cast member was expected to inject some fresh blood into the long-running reality franchise. Now ABC won’t air her season.

item.title: This diet beats out five others when it comes to cognitive health, study finds
item.description: Two studies have found the DASH diet — either alone or combined with the Mediterranean diet — is a winner for lowering the risk of dementia.

item.title: U.S. weighs lifting oil sanctions on Iran as it hits key Gulf energy sites
item.description: The price of Brent crude, the global oil benchmark, briefly climbed past $117 per barrel before settling around $111 Thursday, as Gulf states reported more attacks on critical infrastructure.

item.title: Trump nomination of Mullin for DHS chief advances out of Senate committee
item.description: A committee voted in favor of sending Sen. Markwayne Mullin’s nomination to lead the Department of Homeland Security to the full Senate for consideration.

item.title: America’s self-assassination is accelerating, George F. Will writes
item.description: The national debt is heading for $40 trillion. Blame “Total Boomer Luxury Communism.”

item.title: A visit to D.C. in today’s On the Record
item.description: Answer now to earn your Thursday points.

item.title: Oil prices soar as Iran targets energy facilities across Persian Gulf
item.description: The price of Brent crude climbed past $115 per barrel as Qatar, Saudi Arabia and Kuwait reported more attacks on infrastructure and the U.S. and Iran traded threats.

item.title: Trump’s image on a gold coin? His hand-picked panel will decide on the idea that another federal committee has refused to consider.
item.description: A federal arts commission was expected to weigh whether to approve a commemorative gold coin featuring Donald Trump on Thursday, the administration’s latest effort to celebrate the president, even as Democrats and members of another federal committee say the idea is deeply inappropriate and potentially illegal.

item.title: Unidentified drones. War fallout. Mullin hearing. Energy prices. Trump coin. Spiking airfares. DASH diet. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: Drones detected over the base where Rubio, Hegseth live raise security concerns
item.description: The drone sightings in Washington come as the United States issued a global security alert for overseas diplomatic posts and locked down several domestic bases because of threats. The military is monitoring potential threats more closely because of the heightened alert level as the U.S. and Israel strike Iran, according to a senior administration official who spoke on the condition of anonymity to discuss sensitive security matters.

item.title: Pentagon seeks more than $200 billion in budget request for Iran war, administration official says
item.description: Some White House officials do not think the Pentagon’s request has a realistic shot of being approved in Congress, the official said. That number would far surpass the costs of the administration’s massive airstrike campaign to date and instead seek to urgently increase production of critical weaponry expended, according to three other people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Five tips for when you’re wide awake at 3 a.m.
item.description: Middle-of-the-night insomnia becomes more common as people age, but it still occurs among everyone from teenagers to 90-year-olds. These techniques might help you fall back to sleep.

item.title: Portland figured out a wonky secret to get cheaper houses built. Other cities are copying.
item.description: The Oregon city legalized six-plexes, tiny houses and backyard homes, and then devised a way to actually get them built.
